rationale: The cited paper appears directly relevant to the citing paper’s core manipulation and domain: both study product-option customization and additive vs subtractive framing (add vs reject/delete), with outcomes on option choice and spending. The citing note explicitly includes “option framing (add vs reject)” as a focal construct and positions its contribution as extending prior sequential/fluency work in customization, so this is plausibly a foundational/theory source rather than an incidental mention.
